papers.title_en: On the Increased and Decreased Connectivity of the Demented Human Brain
papers.authors: Daniel Hegedus, Marton Barnabas Mora, Balint Varga, Vince Grolmusz
papers.evidence: 使用OASIS-3数据集研究痴呆症中脑结构连接变化，与脑连接组分析相关
papers.tldr: 随着脑成像技术发展，大量数据可用于研究痴呆脑连接变化。基于OASIS-3数据集，本文通过结构连接性分析，识别出灰质中连接性显著升高或降低的小区域。结果发现海马体和颞叶精细结构连接性下降，而楔前叶、楔叶和脑岛精细结构连接性反而上升。这一发现挑战了痴呆仅导致连接性下降的传统观点，揭示了脑网络重塑的复杂性，为理解痴呆异质性提供了新视角。
papers.abstract_en: With the enormous advances in cerebral imaging techniques, a large amount of data is available for studying the aging and demented brain. In this contribution, we apply the OASIS-3 dataset for identifying small areas of the human gray matter, which have higher- or lower structural connectivity in dementia and aging. As anticipated, we found that finer structures of the hippocampus and the temporal lobe show decreased connectivity in dementia. More surprisingly, the precuneus, the cuneus, and finer structures in the insula show higher connectivity in dementia than in the healthy state.
